what_it_does: A standalone acceptance test runner for the cc_registry_refresh introspection lane. It verifies build-plan criteria C-REF-1 through C-REF-5, generic-adapter proofs, stamp/cache I/O, flow registration, and settings hook wiring using stubs, monkeypatches, and temp directories — without spawning a live Claude binary.
observations: Runs directly as a script and exits non-zero on any failure.
Maintains global PASS/FAIL lists and prints per-check PASS/FAIL lines via an ok() helper.
C-REF-4 is a hard gate: if banned platform strings appear in engine/rules/discover/adapters, the script aborts before running other tests.
Uses tempfile.mkdtemp and shutil.rmtree for isolated store directories in each test block.
Monkeypatches _store_path, _probe_live_version, and _surface_gap to avoid real binaries and real governance inboxes.
Constructs fake PlatformEntry and CapabilityEntry objects to drive classification without real CLI output.
C-REF-1 is only a partial test: it runs an inline bash snippet that mimics the hook's stale-stamp comparison, not the actual cc_registry_freshness_check.sh.
C-REF-2 uses a fake _surface_gap to capture the surfaced payload and verify default='reject' and stamp-not-advanced behavior.
C-REF-5 imports SessionSupervisor and compares derived transport invariants against the real _build_spawn_cmd head.
Includes explicit [LEAD-queued] comments marking criteria that need a live session for full validation.
C-GENPROOF-known classifies a second cli-help platform; C-GENPROOF-novel expects MissingAdapterError for rest-openapi.
standouts: C-REF-4 gate aborts the entire run on any banned string leak.
C-REF-5 drift gate ties the rule derivation to the actual supervisor spawn command head.
C-REF-2 verifies fail-closed surfacing: default='reject', exactly one surface call, stamp not advanced by run().
C-GENPROOF-known/novel together define the adapter-extension boundary.
conventions: Docstring maps every test block to a named build-plan criterion.
Each block uses try/finally to restore monkeypatched values and clean up temp dirs.
Uses model_dump() for Pydantic-style contract objects and json.dump/json.load for cache files.
Inline bash scripts simulate hook behavior for deterministic CI execution.
Criteria marked [LEAD-queued] are stubbed or partially tested rather than skipped.
concerns: Heavy monkeypatching of internal module functions makes tests brittle to refactors in flows.cc_registry_refresh.
C-REF-1 does not exercise the real cc_registry_freshness_check.sh path; the inline script may diverge from the actual hook.
The 'safe' flag assumption in C-REF-3 empty-diff guard depends on signal_sets not changing; a future hazard/axis addition could break it.
C-GENPROOF-known 'zero new engine code' assertion is passed as True without mechanical verification.
Some ok() calls rely on broad string containment checks that could mask subtle behavioral changes.
notes: A stub-driven acceptance harness that gates the build on anti-leak rules and documents which registry-refresh criteria still require a live lead session.
questions: Does the inline bash hook in C-REF-1 faithfully mirror the real cc_registry_freshness_check.sh logic and error messages?
How is the set of banned strings in C-REF-4 kept in sync with the PG2 policy?
What happens if the real signal_sets later classify --something-safe differently, breaking the empty-diff guard?
How will the [LEAD-queued] criteria be triggered and recorded in CI?
Is there a plan to replace the fake _surface_gap with a real but isolated inbox for C-REF-2?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it covers the listed build-plan criteria, uses stubs for no-live-binary verification, and clearly marks lead-only items.
intention_signals: To keep the introspection layer honest: no platform-specific literals in engine code, no silent empty registry when a novel adapter is missing, transport invariants must track the real spawn command, and the stamp only advances after a surfaced gap is approved.
novelty: A single runnable acceptance script that aborts early on a hard leak gate and explicitly partitions criteria into stub-verifiable CI checks versus live lead-session checks.
contribution: A runnable, criterion-mapped acceptance harness that documents which registry-refresh behaviors can be verified without a live binary and which still need a live session, while enforcing the anti-leak gate and fail-closed surfacing invariants.
summary_for_embedding: tests/introspection_acceptance.py is a standalone acceptance test script for the cc_registry_refresh introspection lane that stubs live binaries and monkeypatches store/probe/surface primitives to verify build-plan criteria including anti-leak gating, version-bump diff surfacing, same-version fast path, transport-invariant drift detection, generic platform adapter boundaries, stamp/cache I/O, flow registration, and settings hook wiring.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a deterministic, no-live-binary acceptance gate that proves the registry-refresh flow's safety invariants and documents which criteria still need a live lead session.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that records PASS/FAIL and prints a result line.
symbols.description: Accepts a test name, boolean condition, and optional detail string. Appends the name to the global PASS or FAIL list, prints a formatted PASS/FAIL line, and includes the detail only when the check fails.

symbols.does: Returns the real SessionSupervisor spawn command head for transport-invariant comparison.
symbols.description: Wraps SessionSupervisor._build_spawn_cmd with claude_bin='claude', resume=None, and fork=False so C-REF-5 can compare rule-derived invariants against the actual command-line head.

symbols.does: Provides a temporary store directory for the C-REF-3 test block.
symbols.description: Returns a path inside the C-REF-3 temp directory. It is monkeypatched onto _refresh_mod._store_path so stamp and cache writes stay isolated from the real store.

symbols.does: Simulates a live version probe returning a fixed fake version.
symbols.description: Returns FAKE_VERSION regardless of platform. Used in C-REF-3 to trigger the same-version fast path without invoking a real binary.

symbols.does: Simulates a version-bump live probe returning a newer fake version.
symbols.description: Returns FAKE_VERSION_NEW. Used in C-REF-3 to create a version bump while paired with a same-row discover_fn that produces an empty diff, exercising the empty-diff raise guard.

symbols.does: Returns a single safe flag row to force an empty diff.
symbols.description: Used in C-REF-3 after a version bump. The returned row matches the prior cache's comparable shape, so the diff is empty and the flow raises a broken-read guard RuntimeError.

symbols.does: Provides a temporary store directory for the C-REF-2 test block.
symbols.description: Returns a path inside the C-REF-2 temp directory and is monkeypatched onto _ref2._store_path to keep stamp and cache writes isolated.

symbols.does: Simulates a live version probe returning the newer version for C-REF-2.
symbols.description: Returns NEW_VER. Combined with a stub discoverer that adds a new flag, it drives the version-bump surfacing path in C-REF-2.

symbols.does: Returns old and new flag rows to produce a non-empty added diff.
symbols.description: Used in C-REF-2 to simulate discovery after a version bump. Returns the existing --debug flag plus a new --new-flag, yielding an 'added' entry in the surfaced gap payload.

symbols.does: Captures surface_gap arguments and returns a fake surfaced identifier.
symbols.description: Records the action_class, payload, and default arguments in _surfaced_calls and returns 's1-cc_registry_gap'. This avoids needing a real governance inbox while verifying surfacing shape and fail-closed default.

symbols.does: Provides a temporary store directory for the stamp-logic test block.
symbols.description: Returns a path inside the STAMP-LOGIC temp directory and is monkeypatched onto _ref_stamp._store_path to test read/write stamp and cache primitives in isolation.

symbols.does: Returns simulated CLI flags for a second cli-help platform.
symbols.description: Used in C-GENPROOF-known to feed classify_platform three rows: a transport invariant (-p), another invariant (--input-format), and a safe flag (--output-file), demonstrating that the existing engine/adapter derives postures for a new platform without new engine code.
